methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $7M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Terranova is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Laurence Allen that has raised $7M to date. The robotics and manufacturing sector includes one public comparable (277810.KQ) and eight private peers, among them Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity. However, no private peers in this universe share Terranova's stage or valuation tier in sufficient number to support a reliable peer-median analysis.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using the $7M in total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.5x multiplier typical of seed-to-next-stage advancement in the robotics sector yields an implied valuation of $18M. This method reflects the expected step-up in value as Terranova progresses from seed toward Series A.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $15M to $20M, centered on $18M. The median reflects the stage-progression calculation, while the low and high bounds represent conservative and optimistic interpretations of that multiplier applied to the raised capital. Key uncertainties include the absence of tightly matched private comparables at seed stage, which limits cross-validation of the 2.5x multiplier for this specific subsector and company profile. Investor conviction should be anchored primarily to company fundamentals and market opportunity rather than peer benchmarking.
